topic: well-drained soil and vine root development

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A vineyard soil drains excess water while retaining sufficient plant-available moisture.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Adequate drainage preserves oxygen in the root zone and reduces prolonged waterlogging, allowing roots to respire and explore the soil.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Root development and balanced vine function are supported; drainage alone does not guarantee wine quality.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Good drainage supports root development by maintaining aeration and avoiding prolonged waterlogging.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
